creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
FYI: I will note that:

https://lists.freebsd.org/archives/freebsd-hackers/2022-April/001022.html

is the start of a sequence of messages of someone trying to
buildkernel their way out of a 12.1-RELEASE to 12.3 and
finding kills lead to: "Can not build kernel on 1GB VM".

2019-Nov-04 was the initial 12.1-RELEASE date and so is
about a month after this bugzilla submittal. (12.1 is
no longer supported as well.)

So it might be evidence that there still was a problem.

The context is old and so the standard messaging report
out of swap space as the reason for the kill, no matter
which of the 4(?) conditions actually initiates the kill.
(Likely the interesting time is earlier then that.)

So far my attempt to help with the classic sysctl settings
for avoiding the kills has not been much help.

I also do not know if there is a type of information from
the failure that might still be of use for supported
releases and stable/12 --given that there is a repeatable
failure context to look at.
